scheme_name: State Government Share Capital Contribution to Co.operative Sugar Factories (Normal Area) (Tribal Area)
page_title: State Government Share Capital Contribution to Co.operative Sugar Factories (Normal Area) (Tribal Area)

details: The scheme "State Government Share Capital Contribution to Co.operative Sugar Factories (Normal Area) (Tribal Area)" introduced by the Agriculture, Farmers Welfare and Cooperation Department, Gujarat, aims to facilitates the establishment of new sugar cooperative factories by providing a 30% share capital contribution from the State Government. The objective is to strengthen the cooperative sugar industry and support economic development in the state.
benefits: Minimum Time Limit (in Years) for Reclaiming Benefits
State Government share capital contribution to Co.operative Sugar Factories
Share contribution by the State Government on the basis of 30% of the project cost to the new sugar co-operative factories to be set up in the State.
To the extent of 30% of the project cost to the concerned Sugar Co-operative Societies who are eligible
eligibility: Newly established sugar co-operative factories shall be eligible for this scheme.

offline: The concerned sugar co-operative societies who are eligible, should send the proposal through the District Registrar of the concerned district to the Director of Sugar, Gujarat State, Gandhinagar.
Office Address and Contact Details of District Registrar:
Office Address and Contact Details of Director Of Sugar:
Dr.Jivraj Mehta bhavan, Block No.8 ,2nd floor, Gandhinagar

documents_required: Project report of sugar co-operative factory including construction/machinery
Member’s share capital collected by sugar co-operative society (including the last balance sheet of the society)
Official details regarding sanctioned project loan
Official documents regarding the ownership of the land of the proposed project of the Sugar Co-operative Society

faqs.question: What is the objective of this scheme?
faqs.answer: The objective of this scheme is to provide financial assistance to newly established sugar cooperative factories in Gujarat by contributing 30% of the project cost as share capital.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Newly established sugar cooperative factories located in Gujarat are eligible to apply for financial assistance under this scheme.

faqs.question: What percentage of the project cost does the State Government contribute?
faqs.answer: The State Government contributes 30% of the total project cost as share capital to eligible sugar cooperative factories.

faqs.question: Are existing sugar factories eligible for this scheme?
faqs.answer: No, only newly established sugar cooperative factories are eligible to apply for the scheme.

faqs.question: Can a privately-owned sugar factory apply for financial assistance under this scheme?
faqs.answer: No, only sugar factories operating under the cooperative model are eligible to apply.

faqs.question: Can a sugar factory apply for this scheme without a sanctioned project loan?
faqs.answer: No, a sanctioned project loan is a mandatory requirement for applying under this scheme.

faqs.question: What is the process for applying to this scheme?
faqs.answer: Eligible sugar cooperative societies must submit their proposal through the District Registrar of the concerned district, which is then forwarded to the Director of Sugar, Gujarat State, Gandhinagar, for approval.

faqs.question: Where can applicants find more information about this scheme?
faqs.answer: Applicants can contact the District Registrar of their respective district or the Director of Sugar, Gujarat State, Gandhinagar, for more information.
